evals.prompt: can you mock up an app for tracking my climbing gym sessions? i want to log each climb (grade, style - boulder/toprope/lead, sent or not), see my progress over the last few weeks, and there should be a little leaderboard with my friends. just want to see what it could look like and click around it
evals.expected_output: One self-contained .html file with hardcoded data, all screens of the main flow clickable (log a climb, progress view, leaderboard), fake in-memory interactions, plus reachable empty/loading/error/overflow states.
assertions: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
Log-a-climb flow works: submitting the form adds a climb with grade, style (boulder/toprope/lead), and sent/not-sent to the visible log
A progress-over-weeks view exists (chart or weekly stats derived from the hardcoded sessions)
A friends leaderboard screen exists with ranked entries
An empty state (a list/screen with no data) is reachable by clicking
A loading state (spinner or skeleton) is reachable, e.g. simulated with setTimeout
An error message or toast is reachable by clicking
An overflow case (too-long text or oversized value) is present so the user can see how the layout copes

evals.prompt: Turn this spec into a clickable prototype I can show the office staff tomorrow. source: ./dispatch-spec.md
evals.expected_output: One self-contained .html file implementing the spec's four screens (intake queue, dispatch board, job detail, end-of-day summary) with the spec's data shape hardcoded, emergency jobs visually loud, long-customer-name overflow handled/shown, and an empty-queue state.
assertions: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
All four screens from the spec exist: intake queue, dispatch board (techs as columns with job cards), job detail, and end-of-day summary
A job can be assigned from the intake queue to a tech and then appears on that tech's board column
Emergency-priority jobs are visually loud (distinct alert/red styling), per the spec's 'unassigned + emergency needs to be LOUD' note
The close-out flow captures the spec's fields: parts used, time on site, and a needs-follow-up flag
The hardcoded data uses the spec's job fields (zone, equipment, priority, status, assigned tech)
A too-long customer name (like the spec's 'Greater Metropolitan Property Management LLC, Building C') is included so overflow handling is visible
An empty intake queue state is reachable and does not look broken, per the spec's 'slow afternoons' note
A loading state and an error message/toast are each reachable by clicking

evals.prompt: show me what an invoicing dashboard for my freelance design business would look like - list of invoices with statuses, create a new invoice, mark one paid, and some kind of monthly revenue chart. make it something i can actually click through, not just a picture
evals.expected_output: One self-contained .html file with invoice list, working create-invoice flow, mark-paid interaction mutating in-memory data, a revenue chart, and reachable empty/loading/error/overflow demo states.
assertions: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
The invoice list shows a status per invoice (e.g. draft/sent/paid/overdue)
The create-invoice flow adds a new invoice to the visible list in memory
Marking an invoice paid changes its status and the change is reflected in the UI (totals and/or chart update)
A monthly revenue chart renders from the hardcoded data
An empty state (a list/screen with no data) is reachable by clicking
A loading state (spinner or skeleton) is reachable, e.g. simulated with setTimeout
An error message or toast is reachable by clicking
An overflow case (too-long text or oversized value) is present so the user can see how the layout copes
